w:eastAsia="Arial" w:hAnsi="Arial"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/><w:rtl w:val="0"/></w:rPr><w:t xml:space="preserve">*A - Added M - Modified D - Deleted II. Software Requirement Specification 1. Overall Requirements 1.1 Context Diagram The Bakery Ingredient Contract &amp; Sales Management System is an integrated software developed to replace manual processes and existing fragmented systems in managing the sales and contract lifecycle of a business specializing in bakery ingredients. The system is designed to support quotation negotiation, contract drafting, approval workflows, electronic customer signatures, secure payment processing, as well as inventory management related to warehouse operations.  With the goal of optimizing the sales and contract management processes, enhancing legal consistency, and ensuring transparent audit trails, the system provides an intuitive interface and powerful functionalities, enabling Sale Staff, Managers, Admin Officers, and Warehouse Staff to perform their tasks efficiently and accurately. Additionally, the system supports Customers and staff members in performing basic operations such as logging in, tracking quotations, signing contracts online, and making online payments with ease and convenience. 1.2 Main Business Processes  [Provide main workflows (business processes) using the swim-lane diagram(s) like sample below] Quotation Process     Contract Approval Process   2.3.3 The names of use cases are always written in the form of a verb followed by an object. Select strong, descriptive names to make it evident from the name that the use case will deliver something valuable for some user. Actor clicks &quot;Add New User&quot; and system displays an empty form.  2. Actor populates mandatory fields (Full Name, Username, Email, Phone, Role) and clicks &quot;Save&quot;.  3. System assigns a default hashed password (&quot;1234&quot;) and sets status to 'ACTIVE'.  6. System persists the user, records the &quot;CREATE&quot; action in Audit Log.  7. Step 2: System extracts transaction metadata fields: Order ID, Total Amount, Current Timestamp, and client IP address, and other parameters Step 3: System automatically constructs the standard VNPay query parameter payload array using system configuration credentials (vnp_TmnCode, vnp_HashSecret). Step 4: System executes a hashing routine using the HMAC-SHA512 algorithm to generate a secure checksum signature string (vnp_SecureHash). Step 5: System appends the calculated hash signature onto the target gateway baseline endpoint string to form the final Payment URL. Step 6: System updates the screen state and automatically redirects the user's browser view to the generated VNPay portal interface. <w:trPr><w:cantSplit w:val="0"/><w:tblHeader Step 4 Amount violates business rules If the calculated transaction amount is less than the gateway minimum processing boundary (10,000 VND), the system will halt the execution. VNPay containing transaction response parameters (vnp_TxnRef, vnp_Amount, vnp_ResponseCode, vnp_SecureHash), extracts all incoming data, and re-calculates the secure hash checksum using the local secret key. Step 2: System matches the newly generated hash directly against the received vnp_SecureHash parameter to ensure data integrity and checks if vnp_ResponseCode equals 00 (Success criteria). Step 3: System queries the database using vnp_TxnRef to verify that the target order exists, its status is currently &quot;Pending&quot;, and the database amount record matches the incoming vnp_Amount exactly. Step 4: System updates the order status row to &quot;Paid&quot; in SQL Server, records the payment timestamp, and returns a raw JSON response to VNPay: {&quot;RspCode&quot;:&quot;00&quot;,&quot;Message&quot;:&quot;Confirm Success&quot;}. <w:trPr><w:cantSplit w:val="0"/><w:tblHeader w:val="0"/></w:trPr><w:tc><w:tcPr><w:tcBorders><w:top the calculated validation hash does not match the received vnp_SecureHash parameter string, the system flags the request as a tampering attempt. System halts processing and logs the source IP address. System returns a JSON error response to VNPay: {&quot;RspCode&quot;:&quot;97&quot;,&quot;Message&quot;:&quot;Invalid Checksum&quot;}. Step 2_Transaction failed from gateway side If the received vnp_ResponseCode is not equal to 00 (e.g., code 24 - user canceled transaction, or code 09 - customer's account lacks sufficient balance), the system transitions the order status to &quot;Failed&quot; in the database. System logs the specific gateway error code into the transaction directory and returns code 00 to acknowledge receipt to VNPay. Step 3_Order reference lookup failure If the incoming tracking ID vnp_TxnRef does not point to any existing order inside the database, the system terminates execution. System returns a JSON error response to VNPay: {&quot;RspCode&quot;:&quot;01&quot;,&quot;Message&quot;:&quot;Order Not Found&quot;}. Step 3_Monetary value mismatch If the incoming monetary field vnp_Amount does not equal the original amount stored within the database record, the system blocks mutations. System returns a JSON error response to VNPay: {&quot;RspCode&quot;:&quot;04&quot;,&quot;Message&quot;:&quot;Invalid Amount&quot;}. Step 3_Order already completed (Duplicate webhook handle) If the targeted order entry inside the database has already been successfully processed and marked as &quot;Paid&quot; previously (e.g., due to a duplicate network call), the system avoids duplicate updates. System returns a JSON response to VNPay: {&quot;RspCode&quot;:&quot;02&quot;,&quot;Message&quot;:&quot;Order already confirmed&quot;}. 2.3  Account Recovery Management   2.3.1 Forgot Password Step 2:  System redirects the view and displays the Forgot Password entry screen containing an Email field input block. Step 3:  User inputs their registered account email address, username and clicks the &quot;Send Verification Code&quot; button. Step 4:  System checking in the database to verify if the inputted email exists, username is correct and is currently active. Step 5:  System generates a secure, randomized 8-digit numeric Verification Code (OTP) and commits it to the database with a strict 5-minute expiration window. Step 6:  System initiates an asynchronous connection to the external Google Gmail SMTP Server securely via TLS (Port 587). Step 7:  System builds a predefined HTML layout email body containing the generated OTP string and transmits it through the Gmail SMTP pipeline to the user's destination email address. registry, the system flags a lookup exception error. System returns an explicit warning message block: &quot;The entered email address is not registered in our system.&quot; The workflow is halted, and the user remains on the entry panel screen. Step 6 Gmail SMTP server communication timeout If the local host fails to establish a secure TCP handshake with the Gmail SMTP server gateway (e.g., due to local internet breakdown or blocked port configurations), a system connection exception is thrown. System intercepts the execution block, terminates the generation state token, and rolls back database states. System prints an alert warning notification for the user: &quot;Failed to transmit verification email. Please try again later or contact system administration.&quot; 2.3.1 Verify OTP and Reset Password  </w:sdtPr><w:sdtContent><w:tbl><w:tblPr><w:tblStyle w:val="Table14"/><w:tblW w:w="9341.0" The entered OTP token is invalid or incorrect If the entered verification code character array does not match the token string cached in the database record, the system blocks mutation processing. System returns an explicit warning alert notification: &quot;Invalid verification code entered. Please double-check your inbox details.&quot; Step 4 The OTP token lifespan has expired If the internal system server timestamp value during execution is greater than the generated expiration timestamp marker (e.g., past the 5-minute lifespan rule), the system flags an expiration alert error. System purges the token parameter from the database row and displays an execution prompt message: &quot;Your verification code has expired. Please request a new code transaction.&quot; Step 5 Password parameter matching or complexity validation failure If the values provided inside the &quot;New Password&quot; text field do not match the contents of the &quot;Confirm New Password&quot; text field exactly, or fail to satisfy system security policies, the process is blocked. Actor clicks &quot;Create Contract&quot; from a specific quotation.  2. System fetches Customer and Quotation details, generating an auto-filled HTML template.  3. System displays the HTML editor form with editable=true.  4. Actor modifies the contractContent and clicks &quot;Save Draft&quot;.  5. System auto-generates the Contract Number (format: [id]/[year]-HĐ) and secure UUID token.  6. Non-Functional Requirements 4.1 External Interfaces This section describes how the  Bakery Ingredient Contract &amp; Sales Management System  communicates with external software, services, and components to ensure end-to-end operational workflows. VNPay Payment Gateway    The system integrates with the VNPay payment gateway to process customers’ online payment transactions. Communication is performed via HTTP/REST using query parameters. Transaction data is secured using the HMAC-SHA512 hashing algorithm with a secret key (HashSecret) to ensure data integrity and prevent tampering. Email Server (Gmail SMTP Server)    To support features such as sending verification codes (OTP) and automated notifications (new contracts, successful payments), the system connects to Google’s SMTP server via the Jakarta Mail API. Communication uses the SMTP protocol over port 587, secured with TLS 1.2 (Transport Layer Security), and authenticates using a Google App Password. Database Management System (Microsoft SQL Server)    All system data, including user information, contracts, quotations, and inventory status, is stored and managed in Microsoft SQL Server. The application server communicates directly with the database over TCP/IP using the Microsoft JDBC Driver, ensuring fast access and data consistency. Web Browser (Client Web Browser)    The user interface is presented in standard web browsers. Data exchange between the server and client uses HTTP/HTTPS. The system also implements real-time notification flows using Server-Sent Events (SSE), allowing the server to proactively push updates to the browser UI without requiring the user to reload the page. 4.2 Quality Attributes 4.2.1  Security Description : The system must protect user details, secure passwords, block unauthorized page requests, and validate inputs to prevent cyber attacks. How it is applied: <w:bottom w:color="auto" w:space="0" w:sz="0" w:val="none"/><w:right w:color="auto" w:space="0" w:sz="0" w:val="none"/><w:between w:color="auto" w:space="0" w:sz="0" w:val="none"/></w:pBdr><w:spacing w:after="0" w:before="240" w:line="276" w:lineRule="auto"/><w:ind w:left="720" w:hanging="360"/><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="Arial" w:cs="Arial" w:eastAsia="Arial" w:hAnsi="Arial"/></w:rPr></w:pPr><w:r w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000"><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="Arial" w:cs="Arial" w:eastAsia="Arial" w:hAnsi="Arial"/><w:b w:val="1"/><w:bCs w:val="1"/><w:sz w:val="20"/><w:szCs w:val="20"/><w:rtl w:val="0"/></w:rPr><w:t xml:space="preserve">SQL Injection Prevention: :  When a customer searches for products using the search box, the system treats whatever they type as plain text. If a malicious user tries to type database commands into the search box, the system safely ignores it  Password Hashing:  When a user registers or changes their password, the database does not store the real password. Instead, it stores scrambled characters. If the database is ever leaked, the passwords remain completely hidden. Page Access Control:  If a Customer attempts to type /create-product directly in their browser URL bar, the system blocks them, logs them out immediately, and displays the login screen. 4.2.2 Performance &amp; Scalability Description:  The system must load pages quickly, handle many users without slowing down, and use server resources efficiently. How it is applied: <w:bottom w:color="auto" w:space="0" w:sz="0" w:val="none"/><w:right w:color="auto" w:space="0" w:sz="0" w:val="none"/><w:between w:color="auto" w:space="0" w:sz="0" w:val="none"/></w:pBdr><w:spacing w:after="0" w:before="240" w:line="276" w:lineRule="auto"/><w:ind w:left="720" w:hanging="360"/><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="Arial" w:cs="Arial" w:eastAsia="Arial" w:hAnsi="Arial"/></w:rPr></w:pPr><w:r w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000"><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="Arial" w:cs="Arial" w:eastAsia="Arial" w:hAnsi="Arial"/><w:b w:val="1"/><w:bCs w:val="1"/><w:sz w:val="20"/><w:szCs w:val="20"/><w:rtl w:val="0"/></w:rPr><w:t xml:space="preserve">Response Time: : When a staff member clicks &quot;Send Contract Notification&quot;, the system initiates the email transfer in the background. The staff member's web browser does not freeze or show a loading spinner; they can immediately continue working on other tasks. Capacity Limit Protection (Pagination) :  When an admin opens a list containing thousands of logs, the website loads the list instantly. This is because the system only fetches and displays 10 lines at a time, allowing the page to load fast regardless of how big the database gets. 4.2.3 Auditability &amp; Traceability Description:  The system must record all user actions that modify critical business assets for later tracking and review. How it is applied: <w:bottom w:color="auto" w:space="0" w:sz="0" w:val="none"/><w:right w:color="auto" w:space="0" w:sz="0" w:val="none"/><w:between w:color="auto" w:space="0" w:sz="0" w:val="none"/></w:pBdr><w:spacing w:after="0" w:before="240" w:line="276" w:lineRule="auto"/><w:ind w:left="720" w:hanging="360"/><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="Arial" w:cs="Arial" w:eastAsia="Arial" w:hAnsi="Arial"/></w:rPr></w:pPr><w:r w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000"><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="Arial" w:cs="Arial" w:eastAsia="Arial" w:hAnsi="Arial"/><w:b w:val="1"/><w:bCs w:val="1"/><w:sz w:val="20"/><w:szCs w:val="20"/><w:rtl w:val="0"/></w:rPr><w:t xml:space="preserve">System Action Logging: : When a Manager approves or rejects a contract draft, the action, user name, and time are recorded. The Admin can open the logs tab to see exactly when and who approved the contract. 4.2.4 Reliability &amp; Fault Tolerance Description : The system must handle errors without crashing and manage third-party service issues gracefully. How it is applied: <w:bottom w:color="auto" w:space="0" w:sz="0" w:val="none"/><w:right w:color="auto" w:space="0" w:sz="0" w:val="none"/><w:between w:color="auto" w:space="0" w:sz="0" w:val="none"/></w:pBdr><w:spacing w:after="0" w:before="240" w:line="276" w:lineRule="auto"/><w:ind w:left="720" w:hanging="360"/><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="Arial" w:cs="Arial" w:eastAsia="Arial" w:hAnsi="Arial"/></w:rPr></w:pPr><w:r w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000"><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="Arial" w:cs="Arial" w:eastAsia="Arial" w:hAnsi="Arial"/><w:b w:val="1"/><w:bCs w:val="1"/><w:sz w:val="20"/><w:szCs w:val="20"/><w:rtl w:val="0"/></w:rPr><w:t xml:space="preserve">Automatic Email Retry: If the internet drops for a second while sending a payment receipt to Gmail, the system waits 5 seconds and tries to send it again automatically. It tries 3 times before listing the email as &quot;Failed&quot; so staff can resend it manually later. Exception Handling:   If a staff member accidentally types letters into a product &quot;Quantity&quot; field and clicks submit, the website displays a friendly message warning them rather than crashing or showing a blank page. 4.2.5 Usability Description : The system must guide users easily through workflows, check for errors, and send notifications. How it is applied: <w:bottom w:color="auto" w:space="0" w:sz="0" w:val="none"/><w:right w:color="auto" w:space="0" w:sz="0" w:val="none"/><w:between w:color="auto" w:space="0" w:sz="0" w:val="none"/></w:pBdr><w:spacing w:after="0" w:before="240" w:line="276" w:lineRule="auto"/><w:ind w:left="720" w:hanging="360"/><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="Arial" w:cs="Arial" w:eastAsia="Arial" w:hAnsi="Arial"/></w:rPr></w:pPr><w:r w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000"><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="Arial" w:cs="Arial" w:eastAsia="Arial" w:hAnsi="Arial"/><w:b w:val="1"/><w:bCs w:val="1"/><w:sz w:val="20"/><w:szCs w:val="20"/><w:rtl w:val="0"/></w:rPr><w:t xml:space="preserve">Helpful Input Messages: When a new customer is signing up, if they enter a phone number that is too short, the form immediately alerts them: &quot;Phone requires exactly 10 digits&quot;, guiding them to complete the form correctly. Navigation Cleanup :  When a Customer logs in, the navigation bar hides staff options (like creation of products or audit logs), ensuring customers only see options related to contract signing, quotations, and payments. 5.
